reviews.author: Noam Akselbant
reviews.text: Muong Hoa Valley was one of the highlights of my trip and I would give it a full 5/5. The hike down from the highway takes around 20 minutes and right away the scenery is breathtaking. Looking out over the terraced rice fields and the village below feels surreal. The colors are so vivid that it almost looks unreal. Once you reach the village you come to the main street where there are small restaurants serving local Vietnamese food and stalls with villagers selling their handmade crafts (the items are mostly the same but it still feels nice to support them). The circular walking path around the rice fields is around 30 minutes. It is not very hard but does go up and down quite a bit, so it is better with comfortable shoes. It is not accessible for wheelchairs or strollers. Along the way we saw chickens, dogs, and even water buffalos out in the rice paddies which added so much to the atmosphere. In September the fields were glowing green and yellow with ripe rice ready for harvest. The weather was sunny when I went, but it can change quickly. Walking alongside the river and being able to step close to the water was magical. At the end you can either bike or walk uphill again to the highway.

reviews.author: Krishin Asnani
reviews.text: The trekking within the mountains gave fascinating perspectives - from top down you see the multitudes of layered rice terraces which feel like the waves emanating from a mountain; fluffy mists across the mountains as if they had sighed; solitary houses with black rooftops separated by distances looked lonesome but self sufficient; the bluish hills in the background provided the somber background for the playful shades of green hills in the foreground; the entire landscape carved and patterned with terraces seemed to be sending inscrutable but frolicsome messages; a gurgling stream that cuts across this landscape provided the soundtrack to these messages; When you reach the vortex of the valley and look up, you notice the engulfing mountains and the sheltering skies in all their moody splendor; up close, you notice that terraces are full of wet and glittering mud and soil for rice and tea; You will run in to members of ethnic tribes on your treks; some tourists opt for house stays with tribes hoping that gives them access to some local essence; I felt the tribes convey the same fire side chats with rotating cast of tourists; what would have intrigued me is to see local Vietnamese tourists engaged in house stays as tribes could educate them about their lineage and heritage.

reviews.author: Juste Feyza
reviews.text: Beside it's the off season for the plantations, the area is beautiful. The high mountains on the side, rice fields in front and clouds above you, it is just a good place to be.

reviews.author: Maria Teresa Chietera
reviews.text: Lovely and quiet hiking spot. Very little tourists but walking in the rice fields you can expect quite a bit of mud.

reviews.author: sulochana udawela
reviews.text: Moung Hoa valley is the place ethinc groups are live in Sapa. Trekking through the villages are really amazing. We rent a bike and had a amazing bike tour

reviews.author: K Bruggeman
reviews.text: Beautiful to drive through this valley with a motorbike! It can be a bit tricky because not all roads are as good as the headways. But it is totally worth it! There are enough places to drink/eat something along the way so you can make this a little roadtrip.

reviews.author: Kh Ong
reviews.text: beautiful place. great host. nice coffee. a must if you are passing by

reviews.author: Tina Nads
reviews.text: It’s a free walking trail through a beautiful countryside valley. But now there are many villagers waiting every corner to sell the same kind of handmade bags and things. It’s a little annoying to skip them. My advice is pretending you don’t speak English is the best way to avoid them.

reviews.author: Manish Gayan
reviews.text: What a beautiful valley with rice terraces everywhere. We walked with a local guide who was very knowledgeable and took us through the most beautiful passages and streams with the best views. We were very lucky as the weather was clear throughout the trek. It's highly recommended if you love nature and trekking. You should wear good trekking shoes as some areas are tricky and slippery.

reviews.author: Katy Phelps
reviews.text: I was doing the 2 hour walk from the city centre back to the village I am staying at and just as I was getting tired I found this place on my way back. The view is absolutely beautiful, definitely worth stopping for. The drinks are very nice and the best part is they had a few puppies running around! Super cute!

reviews.author: Yannick Kogitos
reviews.text: walking throw the rice fields is so amazing but need good legs 😄

reviews.author: EG LF
reviews.text: A trekking heaven. Took me a total of 7hrs to and fro from Sapa Square to the village, then continued to Lao Cai village. Total about 27k steps or 15km to cover the trips. There are few free toilet along the tracks. Do enjoy the scenery... went there during Dec, it is cooling all the way, but views are less attractive compared to if you visit during summer due to mists and foggy at the distance. You glet better blue sky in Aug-Sep.

reviews.author: Pas De Nom
reviews.text: If you do not have time to explore the Hmong village, you can take a motorbike ride all the way here for this view. It is worth the visit. The view is breathtaking. You will never regret it.

reviews.author: Janko Kyselica
reviews.text: We have stayed in Mama Chi Homestay in early April and were trekking around Muong Hoa valley. There are so many treks around here, so if you have the opportunity, stay min. of 3 nights. During our visit, rice terraces were beginning to be flooded.

reviews.author: George Vitebsky
reviews.text: Great picturesque valley surrounded by beautiful hills with paddy fields. The villages are so authentic with such authentic life. There is a feeling that you have arrived in a medieval village. So close to civilization but actually so far. Great experience, highly recommend.

reviews.author: Keri Caldwell
reviews.text: Beautiful views of the village below. I didn't see any tourists in the area. This is a good place to soak up the views or go for a hike up/down the road to the village. No tickets or charge to walk.

reviews.author: Peter Tay
reviews.text: I visited Mường Hoa Valley and was captivated by its serene rice terraces, winding river, and lush landscapes. A peaceful and picturesque retreat perfect for nature lovers.

reviews.author: Aaron Tay
reviews.text: Supposedly good views covered by fog, also locals will follow and talk to you, pushing hard to sell handmade products.

reviews.author: Nadir Shaari
reviews.text: Beautiful sceneries and landscapes. You can stop on many viewpoints along the way to enjoy different views. Sometimes you will find local vendors which are a bit pushy but also very kind and sweet. Definitely recommend!!

reviews.author: J. M. Posma
reviews.text: Amazing trek! It wasn't the wet season yet and therefore we only saw some sprouts and only a few fields with them, but beautiful nonetheless. You are going through the fields, like you've seen in the pictures. We had 3 days in Sa Pa and the treks on day 1 and day 3 were disappointing, but visiting Lao Chai and Ta Van made up for it! If you can only do one day: only do this one (forget about Cat Cat village: it's a tourist trap).

reviews.author: Tran Thanh Tuan
reviews.text: A wonderful place to do trekking and exploring the cultural of ethnic groups and enjoy the view of rice fields in Sapa

reviews.author: Katerina Bergeron
reviews.text: We have tried twice to get there. Once we walked and turned around because the whole valley was foggy. Today we tried on a scooter and turned around again before we reached the valley because of fog. I took some foggy pictures on our way.

reviews.author: Freja Butler
reviews.text: Amazing valley and views, very scenic and nice for walking. Would rate 5 stars but was very hard to fully enjoy the experience as we were flocked by the local ethnic minority women using the most pushy sales techniques I have ever experienced. They followed us around for hours and would not take no for an answer. Seemed like if you had a guide maybe they didn't bother you.

reviews.author: Norm P.
reviews.text: Very nice - well worth the stop. Walking to the village, you come across many amazing sights. The views are terrific.

reviews.author: Pao Ly
reviews.text: Muong Hoa valley is a beautiful place with picturesque terraced fields and Muong Hoa stream. It is an ideal check-in point when traveling. It is an intact, pristine valley that still retains its peace. peace and culture no longer exist in today's modern world.

reviews.author: William THC
reviews.text: If you are in Sapa, this is the valley you should go to. Nice place to trekking from the center of sapa. Through villages and hill slope paddy fields. You will get great views on the way to the valley and beautiful rice terraces.

reviews.author: chan Jame
reviews.text: its a wonderful place for sightseeing... especially for watching those fields. Besides, there are a few restauants and homestay hostels in there. Of course, just for hikingis also good enough.

reviews.author: ping ping
reviews.text: Stunning view at thw submit of the moutain it offers. However, walking up a steep stairs ( 600 steps) could be challenging for the people whom has knee prob

reviews.author: peter torma
reviews.text: A lot of craftsmen offer her/his products at a very good value. You can have a feeling about countryside life in NW Vietnam. Good walking trails, but no signs, so better to get a guide on the spot.

reviews.author: Maybel Chua
reviews.text: Beautiful view and lovely short hike
